commit.message:
fix: agent localhost detection, nginx params, item.params field

Three bugs fixed:

1. agent_deployer.py — duplicate class definition
   The edit tool had appended new code before old code without removing the
   old code. Python used the last (old) class definition, so the localhost
   detection and subprocess execution path were never reached.
   The file now contains only the correct single implementation.

2. Item model missing params field
   Zabbix API requires the params field for calculated, SNMP and HTTP agent
   items. Without it, item.create returns:
     Invalid parameter "/1": the parameter "params" is missing.
   Added params: str = "" to Item model.
   _create_item and _update_item in deployer.py now always send params.

3. nginx.yaml calculated item missing formula
   The nginx.requests.rate item had type: calculated but no params
   (formula). Added:
     params: "rate(/nginx/web.page.get[localhost,/nginx_status,80],1m)"

4. inventory.yaml — corrected IP for zabbixtest3100
   Was 127.0.0.1 (triggers localhost/subprocess mode on depa).
   Changed to hostname zabbixtest3100 so zbx uses SSH to reach the
   actual remote machine.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: local file paths in confluencePages + --debug for run command

Bug 1: extractFromConfluence() crashed with 'no protocol' when
confluencePages contained a local file path (e.g. ./agents/instructions/...)
because confluence.contentByUrl() always calls new URL(), which requires
an http/https scheme. Fixed by checking for http/https prefix; non-URL
entries are now read from the local filesystem via Files.readAllBytes().
Also added null/blank guards to avoid NPE when confluencePages is null.

Bug 2: dmtools --debug run <file> silently ignored --debug because the
'run' case in dmtools.sh was handled before LOG_CONFIG was set, with
log4j2-cli.xml hardcoded. Fixed by moving LOG_CONFIG determination to
before the case block; 'run' now uses $LOG_CONFIG like all other commands.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

[Pipeline] Mobile responsiveness: fix layout issues on Compliance, Operator, and Activity pages (#386)

Closes #382

## Summary
Ensures the landing page and all user-facing pages are responsive and
appropriate for mobile and desktop.

## Changes

### `PRDtoProd/Pages/Compliance.cshtml`
- Added `overflow-x: auto` to `.metrics-bar` so the 5-metric strip
scrolls horizontally on narrow screens instead of crushing each cell to
an unusable width
- Added `min-width: 100px` to `.metric-item` to keep metrics legible
when scrolled

### `PRDtoProd/Pages/Operator.cshtml`
- Added a `@media (max-width: 640px)` breakpoint to `.boundary-grid` so
the 2-column boundary detail panel collapses to a single column on
phones

### `PRDtoProd/Pages/Activity.cshtml`
- Added a `@media (max-width: 480px)` breakpoint to `.log-line` so the
fixed-width 160px timestamp column stacks vertically above the log
content on very small screens

## Pages already responsive (no changes needed)
- **Index (/)**: Hero grid has `@media (min-width: 768px)` breakpoint;
pipeline schematic flows naturally
- **Pipeline (/pipeline)**: Has `@media (max-width: 900px)` and `@media
(max-width: 640px)` breakpoints on stage grid
- **Operator (/operator)**: Metrics grid and main sections use Tailwind
`md:` / `lg:` responsive classes

## Notes
> The issue requested Playwright MCP verification. Playwright MCP is not
available in the agent CI environment; changes were validated by code
inspection against viewport widths documented in the CSS.

Distributed AI agent orchestration system built on top of CAO.

and planned src/ layout with stub packages.

commit.message:
fix: replace deprecated vim.fn.sign_* with nvim_buf_set_extmark

The old vim.fn.sign_define/sign_place/sign_unplace API is deprecated
since Neovim 0.10 and can trigger TUI rendering crashes in Neovim 0.11
(e.g. when entering command mode). Replaced with the modern extmark API
(nvim_buf_set_extmark / nvim_buf_clear_namespace) which is stable,
crash-free, and available since Neovim 0.6.

Additional changes:
- Namespace is lazily created on first use (not at module require time)
  to avoid running Neovim API calls during plugin load
- All sign operations are wrapped with pcall for safety
- Default sign_text changed from emoji '💬' to ASCII '>>' to avoid any
  multi-byte rendering issues in the sign column

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Development (#527)

This pull request introduces several improvements and refactors across
backend Python controllers, frontend Vue components, and configuration
files. The main focus is on standardizing permission checks and settings
retrieval for dashboard charts and number cards, enhancing dynamic HTML
component communication, and enforcing stricter validation for
customizing DocTypes. Additionally, UI adjustments improve card
rendering and overflow handling.

**Backend: Permission Checks and Validation**

* Refactored `chart_settings` and `number_card_settings` functions to
use centralized permission and settings checks via
`Chart.check_chart_permissions_and_settings` and
`NumberCard.check_card_permissions_and_settings`, simplifying logic and
ensuring consistent permission handling.
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-b31463e5509e8762327667139c1595cdb274a647f8e5f4c63066515edd15a43fL109-R113)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-91421d7afaf3a01cee8c19677ea0f1220d084c58d498591e6dfdcd6a8ccb4fcdR57-R63)
* Added the `SVACustomizeForm` class to override and strictly validate
which DocTypes can be customized, preventing customization of core,
single, or custom DocTypes. Registered this override in `hooks.py`.
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-a89171bce7e2fbb571f0c1e4b8ed6f8b7008388538a046169d674d975751eaffR1-R18)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-adfb0f36c31e6e5b0c32abffc27f782ebdaf34a2e662496b155fcae75532756dL152-R155)
* Improved file upload integration by skipping folders and files without
a valid path, preventing unnecessary cloud upload attempts.

**Frontend: Dynamic HTML & Card Components**

* Enhanced the dynamic HTML Vue component (`App.vue`) to support
refetching and download requests from iframe content, passing extra
parameters for data fetching, and updating sandbox attributes for better
popup handling.
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-93e47c2da9f04e4d11f77443f9d21ec8a8b265996ea828e6eeff1e84f66a11dfR36-L37)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-93e47c2da9f04e4d11f77443f9d21ec8a8b265996ea828e6eeff1e84f66a11dfR48-R66)
[[3]](diffhunk://#diff-93e47c2da9f04e4d11f77443f9d21ec8a8b265996ea828e6eeff1e84f66a11dfL73-R85)
* Improved the `NumberCard.vue` component and its styles to better
handle overflow scenarios, ensuring card labels and content are properly
truncated and visually consistent.
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-ec302968b8de1a1b58f4aae395de3e0032ea32ff03b8a825bda741c75b0db2d7L3-R9)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-ec302968b8de1a1b58f4aae395de3e0032ea32ff03b8a825bda741c75b0db2d7R297-R304)

**Configuration and Metadata**

* Updated `sva_task_planner.json` to add conditional mandatory field
logic for repeat fields, making them mandatory only when not grouped,
and updated metadata timestamps.
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-78942a70afd8b5ed8cf1ba03173acd2cda890f8927629f9fc0f78827a554218eL66-R67)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-78942a70afd8b5ed8cf1ba03173acd2cda890f8927629f9fc0f78827a554218eR137-R141)
[[3]](diffhunk://#diff-78942a70afd8b5ed8cf1ba03173acd2cda890f8927629f9fc0f78827a554218eL167-R170)

commit.message:
fix: LogViewer reopen after close + preserve text selection

Bug 1: Window couldn't be reopened after closing. The defer order
caused w.Destroy() to run after logViewerOpen was already reset to
false, leaving a gap where a second ShowLogViewer() call would find
logViewerOpen=false but the old WebView was still alive. Fixed by
calling w.Destroy() explicitly before the state-reset defer.

Bug 2: Text selection was destroyed every 2 seconds by auto-refresh
replacing innerHTML. Added hasUserSelection() guard that skips DOM
replacement when the user has an active selection in the log area.
User-initiated actions (search, filter, clear) use force=true to
bypass the guard. Also added user-select:text and cursor:text to
the log area CSS.

fix: exploration phase - generalized URL discovery and filtering

Root causes found and fixed:
1. controller.py: Remove auto-inject of analyze_links results into new_links
   - Every analyze_links call was silently adding ALL links to target_urls
   - Agent's report_urls() filtering was completely bypassed (52 vs 12 targets)

2. analysis.py: Generalize _categorize_link with nav/detail heuristics
   - Add 'nav' category for universal utility paths (login, trending, about, etc.)
   - Recognize /pen/, /repo/, /project/ as detail patterns
   - 3-segment paths (user/type/id) categorized as 'detail' for content platforms
   - analyze_links now smart-truncates (detail-first, max 40) when >30 links

3. orchestrator.py: Replace page_html content-check in report_urls with
   URL structure validation (generic, works for any site, SPA-safe)
   - Reject: root URLs, known nav single-segment paths
   - Accept: 3+ segment depth, known detail keywords, numeric IDs

4. context.py: Exploration prompt teaches agent to use category field
   from analyze_links and explicitly calls out report_urls() only

E2E result: Phase 1 now 3-7 steps (was 17+), reports 12 clean pen URLs
(was 184 noise). Phase 2 processes real pen pages. 132 records from 6 pages.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: 2FA support for HA addon login (#46)

* feat: add 2FA support for HA addon authentication

Replace the Supervisor /auth shortcut (which bypasses MFA) with a
full HA Core login_flow API call (POST /auth/login_flow). This
properly supports TOTP/2FA configured on the HA user account.

Flow:
1. Submit username+password → HA starts the login flow
2. If user has 2FA enabled, HA returns step_id='mfa'
3. Login form switches to TOTP code input
4. Code is submitted to complete the flow
5. On create_entry response → session authenticated

If HA Core is unreachable (homeassistant:8123), falls back to the
old Supervisor /auth endpoint (no 2FA, same as before).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: replace keepalive checkbox sub-row with inline interval field

Move the keep-alive configuration from a separate sub-row (checkbox
+ hidden interval input) into an inline 'KA s' column in the main
device grid row.

- 0 = disabled (default)
- Value ≥ 30 = enabled with that interval in seconds
- Values 1–29 are automatically clamped to 30 on save
- Server-side validation updated: 0 or 30–3600 (was 10–300)
- sendspin_client.py: keepalive_enabled derived from interval > 0;
  legacy keepalive_silence bool still accepted for backward compat

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address Copilot review comments on HA 2FA auth flow

- Guard empty flow_id from _ha_flow_start(): fall back to Supervisor
  auth instead of POSTing to /auth/login_flow/ with empty flow_id
- Separate flow_id-missing vs code-missing on MFA step: expired flow
  restarts login; missing code keeps user on MFA step with error
- Include mfa_module_id in MFA step payload sent to HA Core
- Normalize TOTP code (strip spaces/dashes) before sending
- Tighten input pattern to [0-9]{6} with maxlength=6 (was [0-9 ]*)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address second round of Copilot review comments

- rstrip('/') on _HA_CORE_URL to avoid double-slash URLs when the
  env var is provided with a trailing slash
- Distinguish network failures from HTTP errors in _ha_flow_start():
  HTTPError -> {"_ha_error": True} (HA is up, MFA bypass prevented)
  network/timeout -> None (HA unreachable, Supervisor fallback is safe)
- Update login() caller: _ha_error or missing flow_id -> show
  'service unavailable', no Supervisor fallback (no MFA bypass risk)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore: bump version to 2.7.6

commit.message:
Fix history disappearing: use threading.Lock instead of flag

Replace _search_in_progress flag (which had a timing hole because it was
cleared at the start of display_results, before results_view was actually
cleared) with a threading.Lock that guards the entire clear→fill→snapshot
sequence in display_results.

In tab_changed, use a non-blocking acquire: if display_results is
mid-update the lock is held, so we skip the snapshot and keep the last
valid search_controls. This eliminates the race window entirely.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: localhost mode + generic multi-script agent deployer

## Localhost detection (skip SSH)

When a host's IP is 127.0.0.1, localhost, ::1, or 0.0.0.0, zbx now
detects this and skips SSH entirely. All commands run via subprocess
on the local machine. This means zbx agent deploy/diff/test work
out-of-the-box on the Zabbix server itself without any SSH setup.

  _LOCALHOST = frozenset({'127.0.0.1', 'localhost', '::1', '0.0.0.0'})
  connect() → if _is_localhost(ip): log and return  (no paramiko)
  _run()    → if self._local: subprocess.run(shell=True)
              else: ssh.exec_command()

## Generic multi-script support

The deployer is fully script-agnostic. Any number of scripts and
UserParameter groups can be defined per host — S3, nginx, disk, API,
custom checks, etc. Each entry is handled independently:
  - SHA-256 diff per script → only redeploy changed files
  - One .conf file per UserParametersFile entry
  - Groups related parameters together (one domain per file)

## agent_deployer.py refactor

- Removed unused  and TYPE_CHECKING imports
- Added  frozenset +  helper
-  — no-op for localhost, paramiko for remote
-  — subprocess (local) or SSH exec_command (remote)
-  — hashlib (local) or remote sha256sum
-  — Path.read_text (local, falls back to sudo cat)
                   or remote cat (SSH)
-  — shutil.copy2+sudo mv (local) or sftp+sudo mv (remote)
-  — unified for both modes

## inventory.yaml

- Explicit comment: ip: 127.0.0.1 → localhost, no SSH
- Shows how to add multiple scripts (check_api.py, lld-disks.py, etc.)
- Shows how to add multiple userparameter groups (s3monitor, nginx, etc.)
- Added remote host example in comments

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: wrap job on_exit callback in vim.schedule

jobstart on_exit can fire while Neovim is in an unsafe state (e.g.
during cmdline processing), causing a hard crash when the callback
immediately calls Neovim API functions like vim.notify, sign_place, etc.

Wrapping the callback dispatch in vim.schedule defers all API calls to
the next safe iteration of the main event loop.

Also guard collect() against nil/empty data tables to prevent a
nil-concat crash on edge-case Neovim builds.

feat: zbx agent — deploy scripts and UserParameters via SSH

Closes the loop between Zabbix server config (templates/items/triggers)
and the monitored-host agent config (scripts + UserParameters).

## Problem
Deploying Zabbix monitoring previously required manual steps on each host:
  sudo chown zabbix:zabbix /usr/local/scripts/zabbix/getS3Storage.py
  sudo vi /etc/zabbix/zabbix_agentd.d/userparameters_s3monitor.conf

There was no way to track these changes in Git or roll them out consistently.

## Solution: zbx agent

  zbx agent diff   <host>   Show what would change (no SSH writes)
  zbx agent deploy <host>   SSH in, deploy scripts + write userparams
  zbx agent test   <host>   Run zabbix_agentd -t for each test_key

## How it works

1. Scripts are stored in scripts/ in the repo (versioned in Git)
2. zbx computes SHA-256 of local vs remote — only redeploys on change
3. UserParameters .conf files are generated from YAML — no manual editing
4. chown/chmod/mkdir all happen via sudo over SSH
5. Optionally restarts zabbix-agentd after deploy

## New files

zbx/agent_deployer.py
  AgentDeployer: connect, diff, deploy, test_keys, close
  AgentDiff dataclass: scripts[], userparameters[], has_changes
  _render_userparameters(): YAML → UserParameter=key,command lines

zbx/commands/agent.py
  Typer sub-app: diff, deploy, test

scripts/README.md
  Documents the scripts/ directory convention

## Model changes (models.py)

  UserParameter    key + command (one UserParameter= line)
  UserParametersFile  name, path, parameters[]
  ScriptDeploy     source, dest, owner, group, mode
  AgentConfig      ssh_user, ssh_port, ssh_key, sudo, scripts[],
                   userparameters[], restart_agent, test_keys[]
  InventoryHost    + agent: Optional[AgentConfig]

## inventory.yaml updated

Full agent: block added for zabbixtest3100 with:
  - scripts/getS3Storage.py → /usr/local/scripts/zabbix/
  - userparameters_s3monitor.conf with s3.user.discover + s3.user.metrics[*]
  - test_keys: [s3.user.discover]

## pyproject.toml

Added paramiko>=3.4 as dependency.

## Full workflow

  git add scripts/getS3Storage.py
  zbx agent diff   zabbixtest3100        # preview
  zbx agent deploy zabbixtest3100        # deploy
  zbx agent test   zabbixtest3100        # verify
  zbx apply configs/s3-monitoring/       # Zabbix server side
  zbx apply configs/hosts/               # template linking + macros

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
